title: Pseudohemipilia pilosa
intro: Pseudohemipilia pilosa, also known as Himalayan Snow Lotus or Himalayan Pseudohemipilia, is a type of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. The health benefits of Pseudohemipilia pilosa include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pseudohemipilia pilosa is often prepared as a tea, extract, or tincture, and is used to promote relaxation, calmness, and overall well-being.
taxonomy: Pseudohemipilia pilosa is classified as a species within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family comprises approximately 30,000 species of flowering plants, making it one of the largest and most diverse plant families. Pseudohemipilia pilosa is native to the Himalayan region and parts of Asia, and it is a terrestrial orchid. Within the Orchidaceae family, Pseudohemipilia pilosa is classified under the subfamily Orchidoideae, which includes many terrestrial orchids.
